    1. The Question You Gotta Be Able To Answer In Your Sleep
</h2>

<p></p><h2> <strong>"If you don't believe in <em>something</em>, you'll go for anything." Alexander Hamilton</strong>
</h2><p><br></p><p>There are sixty billion "messages" sent out on digital platforms each day.</p><p>How do YOU plan to stand out?</p><p>People obsessively try to grow their "following" but rarely stop and think about the message they'd like to communicate to this "audience" they're desperately trying to grow. </p><p>But think of people you respect and are influenced by. They have a message. They represent something. That's why you love them. That's why you're influenced by them.</p><p><em>Even the president you may or may not hate has a “message”. And that's why you're weirdly triggered by him.</em></p><p>Everyone else? Playing in the middle. Trying not to say anything too bold or alienating.</p><p>Well that's totally fine if you're a "worker". But it's NOT fine if you're an artist... or if you're a brand trying to sell something.</p><p><strong>If you can't stand on a stage and tell an audience why what you do is important... then you don't have a message. Whether you're a model, a restaurant, a television show or an insurance company... when you have a message -- it shows.</strong></p><p>Even in this visual era we're living in... Words are more important than pictures if you want to build a solid foundation for your business.</p><p>I can show you 1 amazing picture of an athlete performing an incredible physical feat:</p><p><br></p><p><img data-imageloader="" data-imageloader-src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/PgBYZt00SkSYkG75MBT7_ae70fcc9-5270-4eb2-9aa1-1e94e81040fc.png" src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/PgBYZt00SkSYkG75MBT7_ae70fcc9-5270-4eb2-9aa1-1e94e81040fc.png" data-imageloader-current-size="" class="is-loaded"></p><p><br></p><p>But you'll have no clue what brand it is until I add the words "Just Do It"... </p><p><br></p><p><img data-imageloader="" data-imageloader-src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/Os3uucnOTCDvyzyKCt7v_50d6cabb-9b77-4753-927a-e0db88d0e807.png" src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/Os3uucnOTCDvyzyKCt7v_50d6cabb-9b77-4753-927a-e0db88d0e807.png" data-imageloader-current-size="" class="is-loaded"></p><p><br></p><p>or maybe...</p><p>"It Gives You Wings" instead.</p><p><br></p><p><img data-imageloader="" data-imageloader-src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/R6Vw6nuDRU2Cb5uYhKqw_3a9694e8-ec76-4d39-8266-576327a5feb0.png" src="https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/R6Vw6nuDRU2Cb5uYhKqw_3a9694e8-ec76-4d39-8266-576327a5feb0.png" data-imageloader-current-size="" class="is-loaded"></p><p><br></p><p>Powerful slogans and symbols all derive from the brand's message.</p><p><strong>The message is how people know who they're dealing with. </strong></p><p>Powerful messages incite people into passionate action.</p><p>Action like... buying... or listening... or getting involved. </p><p><em>But without a message, you will continuously have a crisis of faith.</em></p><p>Native Americans have a thing called "vision quest" where they send their young that are coming-of-age into the woods without food and water for like 4 days. They're instructed to use that time to pray to the Gods until they get their vision – the vision that will tell them their purpose in life, their role in the community, and how they should serve the people. If they can't find it... They're not allowed back.</p><p><em>Every business needs to go through their own "vision quest". </em></p><p>Oh, I've done it with people... I've SEEN the difference.</p><p>Sometimes it took 2 days. Sometimes it took 5.</p><p>Always intense... soul-searching sessions.</p><p>But when you find your message... you literally wanna cry. It's life-changing. What you "need for the video" is obvious now. How to handle social media is suddenly crystal clear. Every new idea can now be measured against something meaningful.</p><p><strong>When you have a message all your actions are guided by a different logic.</strong></p><p>A bad day working on your mission feels better than a good day would in the old world when you didn't know what you stood for.</p><p>But sorry, there are really no shortcuts. You can't get away with "surface" messages like "I want to spread love", or "I want to help people". </p><p>Clichés aren't messages.</p><p><em>Your message has to show us a new version of the world that had been missing until YOU arrived. </em> </p><p><strong>It takes deep work to find your real message. You know... A message derived from your deepest values and principles. </strong></p><p>Most marketing agencies aren't prepared or willing to dive into that process with you.</p><p>You may have to do it on your own.</p><p>But if you're committed to getting help... Here are the three types of agencies you'll have to sort through in the wild:</p><p>1. The “check all the boxes” type that will help you generate middle-of-the-road “content” to feed the beast. </p><p>2. The "shiny new objects" type with all the latest hacks and tricks to manipulate your potential customers.</p><p>3. The "all ideas must serve the message" type who almost feels like they're part of the family because they're so aligned with your values.</p><p>I hope you find a #3.</p><p>'Cause remember... </p><p><em>A marketing agency’s philosophy is a macrocosm: however they encourage you to treat your customers is ultimately the same way that they will end up treating you. </em></p><p><em>What's the only way you can get away with not having a message? Achieve mastery of the core principles in LE$$XN 02... </em></p><p></p>
</div>
